skill.name: surveillance-monitoring
skill.description: Monitor Ubiquiti Protect surveillance cameras and events. Track camera status, review recordings and alerts, and monitor system health.
skill.summary: Monitor Ubiquiti Protect cameras, events, recordings, connectivity, storage, and system health.
skill.author: DataKnifeAI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks as shell execution and the description line as weak cryptography. Review found these are false positives in documentation text, with no executable code, prompt injection, network calls, or secret access in the scanned file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Tool References
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged inline backticks around tool names as Ruby or shell execution. These lines are Markdown documentation that names available tools and workflow steps; no command execution syntax is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is Markdown inline code used to document tool names. No shell, Ruby, or executable command context appears around these lines.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged the metadata description as weak cryptography. The line contains only a natural-language skill description and does not reference a cryptographic algorithm, hash function, or security API.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed line is plain frontmatter description text. I found no code or cryptographic operation in the scanned skill file.

content.value_statement: Security teams need fast visibility into camera status, events, recordings, and storage health. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Ubiquiti Protect monitoring workflows.
actual_capabilities: List Ubiquiti Protect cameras and review their status.
Check camera connectivity and identify offline devices.
Review recent surveillance events and alerts.
Verify recording status for cameras and events.
Summarize Protect system health and storage usage.
Generate surveillance audit reports from available Protect data.
limitations: Requires access to compatible Protect and network tools.
Cannot inspect camera video unless connected tools provide that data.
Does not replace legal, privacy, or physical security review.
Results depend on current device data and retention settings.

use_cases.title: Daily Camera Health Review
use_cases.description: Check all cameras for connectivity, recording status, storage pressure, and urgent alerts before business hours.

use_cases.title: Incident Event Review
use_cases.description: Review recent motion, person, or alert events and confirm that matching recordings exist.

use_cases.title: Coverage Audit Preparation
use_cases.description: Summarize camera locations, status, and possible coverage gaps before a site security audit.

prompt_templates.title: Check Camera Status
prompt_templates.prompt: Show all Protect cameras, their online status, recording status, and any cameras that need attention.

prompt_templates.title: Review Recent Events
prompt_templates.prompt: Review surveillance events from today. Group them by event type and highlight events with missing recordings.

prompt_templates.title: Audit System Health
prompt_templates.prompt: Check Protect system health, storage usage, camera connectivity, and recording coverage. Summarize risks and recommended actions.

prompt_templates.title: Investigate Coverage Gaps
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare camera status, known camera locations, and recent event patterns. Identify possible coverage gaps and maintenance priorities.

output_examples.output: The response lists each offline camera, last known status time, affected area, and suggested next checks.
The response summarizes event counts, notable timestamps, affected cameras, and whether recordings are available.
The response provides a concise report covering camera health, storage status, recording coverage, alerts, and action items.

best_practices: Run camera health checks on a regular schedule.
Review storage capacity before retention limits affect recordings.
Document camera locations and maintenance actions.
anti_patterns: Do not use surveillance summaries as the only incident record.
Do not ignore privacy, consent, and retention requirements.
Do not assume missing events mean no activity occurred.

faq.question: What systems does this skill support?
faq.answer: It is written for Ubiquiti Protect workflows and depends on available Protect and network tools.

faq.question: Can it show live video?
faq.answer: No. The skill describes monitoring workflows and can only use data exposed by connected tools.

faq.question: Can it find offline cameras?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides the assistant to list cameras, check connectivity, and highlight offline or underperforming devices.

faq.question: Can it verify recordings?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide checks for recording status and event recording availability when the tools provide that information.

faq.question: Is this a compliance tool?
faq.answer: No. It can support audits, but compliance decisions require qualified review of laws, policies, and evidence.

faq.question: What should users provide?
faq.answer: Users should specify time ranges, camera groups, event types, and areas that need review.
